description_long: This object consists of two wooden baseball bats crossed together, each featuring a distinct wrapped section around the barrel. One bat is wrapped with a dark, metallic wire, while the other is wrapped with a reddish, possibly blood-stained barbed wire, giving them a rugged, improvised appearance. The wood is dark brown and appears worn, with visible grain and some scuff marks, suggesting heavy use. The handles are smooth and taper to a rounded knob at the end, typical of traditional baseball bats. The wrapped sections are tightly coiled and cover a significant portion of the upper barrel, adding a menacing, weapon-like quality. No visible logos, manufacturer names, or decorative markings are present on the bats.
description: Two dark wooden baseball bats crossed, each with a wire-wrapped barrel—one with metallic wire, the other with reddish barbed wire, giving a rugged, improvised look.
description_view_0: Front view showing both bats crossed, clearly displaying the contrasting wire wrappings on each barrel.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the length and curvature of the bats, with the wire details more visible on the upper surfaces.
description_view_2: Top-down view emphasizing the symmetry of the crossed bats and the tightness of the wire wrappings.
description_view_3: Rear angled view showing the handles and the back side of the wire-wrapped barrels.
